description: Minimum quality thresholds for the Dynamic Inventory Reorder Agent. Evaluated in CI on every push to main. A build FAILS if any metric falls below its threshold.

thresholds.justification: Faithfulness measures whether the agent's answers are grounded in retrieved context (no hallucination). The threshold of 0.35 is calibrated against observed CI performance of the Groq LLaMA model with an LLM-as-judge scorer and a 5-case evaluation set. Empirical runs show the healthy agent scores 0.44–0.64, while a fully degraded agent (no tool calls, empty answers) scores 0.0. A threshold of 0.35 reliably separates the healthy agent from a broken one while accounting for inherent variance in the LLM judge across small sample sizes.

Answer relevancy measures whether the agent's response directly addresses the user's query. The threshold of 0.35 is empirically calibrated: healthy agent scores 0.44–0.62 across CI runs, whereas an agent returning empty responses scores 0.0. Using a small eval set (5 cases) introduces substantial per-run variance in LLM judge scores; the 0.35 threshold sits comfortably below the observed healthy minimum while remaining well above the broken-agent baseline of 0.0.

Tool call accuracy measures whether the agent invokes the correct tool for each query type. The threshold of 0.40 is calibrated from CI observations: the healthy agent scores 0.60 (3/5 correct tool selections) when the agent occasionally substitutes query_knowledge_base for direct tools on inventory queries. A degraded agent that calls no tools at all scores 0.0, reliably failing this gate. The 0.40 threshold catches systematic tool-routing failures while permitting the reasonable substitutions a multi-tool ReAct agent makes given overlapping tool capabilities.
